questionText:
Match List - I with List - II.

List – I
Reagents		 List – II
Reaction Name (Involving aldehydes)
A.  H2, Pd–BaSO4
B.  SnCl2, HCl
C.  CrO2Cl2, CS2
D.  CO, HCl, Anhyd. AlCl3 		I.  Etard Reaction
II.  Rosenmund Reduction
III.  Gattermann–Koch Reaction
IV.  Stephen Reaction

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
subject: Chemistry
solution:
A. $H_2$, Pd–BaSO$_4$ is used to reduce acyl chlorides to aldehydes (poisoned catalyst) $\Rightarrow$ Rosenmund Reduction (II).

B. SnCl$_2$, HCl reduces nitriles to iminium salt which on hydrolysis gives aldehyde $\Rightarrow$ Stephen Reaction (IV).

C. CrO$_2$Cl$_2$ in CS$_2$ oxidises benzylic methyl group $(\text{-CH}_3)$ to aldehyde $(\text{-CHO})$ $\Rightarrow$ Etard Reaction (I).

D. CO, HCl, anhyd. AlCl$_3$ formylates aromatic ring to give an aldehyde $\Rightarrow$ Gattermann–Koch Reaction (III).

So the correct matching is:

$$A\!-\!II,\; B\!-\!IV,\; C\!-\!I,\; D\!-\!III$$

Correct option: D.

B.  SnCl2, HCl

D.  CO, HCl, Anhyd. AlCl3 		I.  Etard Reaction
II.  Rosenmund Reduction
III.  Gattermann–Koch Reaction
IV.  Stephen Reaction

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
subject: Chemistry
solution:
A. $H_2$, Pd–BaSO$_4$ is used to reduce acyl chlorides to aldehydes (poisoned catalyst) $\Rightarrow$ Rosenmund Reduction (II).

B. SnCl$_2$, HCl reduces nitriles to iminium salt which on hydrolysis gives aldehyde $\Rightarrow$ Stephen Reaction (IV).

C. CrO$_2$Cl$_2$ in CS$_2$ oxidises benzylic methyl group $(\text{-CH}_3)$ to aldehyde $(\text{-CHO})$ $\Rightarrow$ Etard Reaction (I).

D. CO, HCl, anhyd. AlCl$_3$ formylates aromatic ring to give an aldehyde $\Rightarrow$ Gattermann–Koch Reaction (III).

So the correct matching is:

$$A\!-\!II,\; B\!-\!IV,\; C\!-\!I,\; D\!-\!III$$

Correct option: D.

questionText: What is the correct order of acidity of the protons marked $$\mathrm{A}-\mathrm{D}$$ in the given compounds ?
subject: Chemistry
solution:
Acidity of an acid depends upon the stability of it's conjugate base.

HC > HD > HA > HB

HC is hydrogen of carboxylic acid.

HD removal will lead to stable carbanion.
